summary: Iteration 5 fully tested (backend + frontend). All 11 new backend tests pass and all 27 iteration-1..3 regression tests still pass. The 2 new endpoints (/api/me/accounts CRUD, /api/analytics/platform/{platform}) and the 2 new pages (/services/:platform and /my-accounts) work end-to-end. Dashboard 'Jump into a platform' tiles, Landing page platform Links, NewOrder URL preset (?platform / ?service / ?link) and the new 'My Accounts' sidebar entry are all in place. Admin routes + 'Import services' button still present. No critical regressions. Two minor UX/test-affordance notes only.

issues: Account delete uses window.confirm() native browser dialog — feels jarring in an otherwise polished design system. Recommend swapping to shadcn AlertDialog for consistency with the rest of the app.

action_items: (Optional UX) Replace window.confirm in MyAccounts.jsx delete handler with an AlertDialog for consistency.
(Optional testability) Add data-testid='delete-account-{account_id}' to the Trash button in /my-accounts cards. Currently it's reachable only via the lucide-trash-2 SVG class.

(Cleanup) A leftover tracked account '@iter5e2e' is in the demo user's accounts from this test run (delete dialog was dismissed in the first attempt). Either delete via UI or via DELETE /api/me/accounts/{id}. Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/app/accounts_router.py — list_accounts uses {'_nope': True} as a no-op match in $or to avoid empty $or, which is correct, but the regex stage also runs against orders.link with the @-stripped handle and is case-insensitive. This will FALSE-POSITIVE-match if a different user link contains the substring (e.g., handle 'demo' would match any link containing 'demo'). Mitigation: stats are scoped per user_id+platform so blast radius is limited, but consider also requiring word boundaries or handle uniqueness per user.

MyAccounts.jsx (line 18) — PLATFORMS = Object.keys(ICONS) drives the Add-account select, but PlatformView/ServicesBrowse PLATFORM_META has its own list of 16 platforms. Keep these two lists in sync (extract a single shared module).

accounts_router.add_account does NOT enforce uniqueness on (user_id, platform, handle). A user can add the same handle 100 times, each creating a separate doc. Recommend a unique compound index OR a 409 if duplicate.
PlatformView pre-renders 62 'Order now' buttons for Instagram (no pagination/virtualisation). Fine today (~62 services) but will become heavy as catalog grows past 200.

/api/analytics/platform/{platform} does NO server-side validation that 'platform' is one of the known 16 — an unknown value just returns zeros + services_total=0. Acceptable but consider 404 for cleanliness.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the iteration-5 features list; all 10 sampled regression pages load without errors

seed_data_creation: Test placed 1 instagram order on demo (link https://instagram.com/test_iter5_demo) to verify analytics correlation. Created+deleted account '@test_iter5_demo'. UI test created+deleted '@uitest_iter5'. Leftover '@iter5e2e' tracked account remains on demo user (action_item).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 is GREEN. /api/orders/export 404 from iteration 4 was NOT re-tested in this run (out of scope for iter 5) — main agent should confirm whether it was fixed in this iteration or carried over. New endpoints to remember: GET/POST/DELETE /api/me/accounts and GET /api/analytics/platform/{platform}. Run regression as: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py.
rca of the issue: n/a — no failing tests in this iteration.
